text: The Future of AI is widely debated, with many experts focusing on the arrival of Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI. Unlike current AI, which can only perform specific tasks, AGI would be a general intelligence, capable of learning and understanding any intellectual task just like a human. Potential benefits are enormous: from solving climate change and curing diseases to improving education and productivity. However, there are serious risks, including job displacement and the challenge of ensuring AGI acts safely and ethically. While some scientists predict AGI could emerge within the next ten to twenty years, others believe it will take much longer. This uncertainty makes careful planning essential.
